title: 反時間管理-Anti-Time Management Reclaim Your Time and Revolutionize Your Results with the Power of Time Tipping

書名頁 - Title Page
版權 - Copyright
目錄 - Table of Contents
獻詞 - Dedication
題詞 - Epigraph
前言 時間 = 今天是我的一切 - Preface TIME = Today Is My Everything
導言：為什麼要反時間管理？ - Introduction Why Anti-Time Management?
第一部分 目的：停止管理時間，開始優先處理注意力 - PART I Purposes: Stop Managing Time, Start Prioritizing Attention
第一章 讓最終因成為你的首因：如何選擇何時做什麼 - Chapter 1 Make Final Cause Your First Cause: How to Choose What to Do and When
第二章 為彈性而非平衡工作：如何掌控你的時間 - Chapter 2 Work for Flexibility, Not Balance: How to Own Your Time
第三章 建造城堡，然後建造護城河：如何釋放你的時間並保護它 - Chapter 3 Build the Castle, Then the Moat: How to Free Up Your Time and Then Protect It
第二部分 實踐：從分心轉向行動 - PART II Practices: Move from Distraction to Action
第四章 堆疊你的專案——專案堆疊：如何讓時間為你工作 - Chapter 4 Stack Your Projects—Project Stacking: How to Make Time Work for You
第五章 同步你的工作——工作同步：如何將工作與生活同步 - Chapter 5 Sync Your Work—Work Syncing: How to Sync Your Work with Your Life
第六章 外包給專家——專家採購：如何在不懂的情況下做任何事 - Chapter 6 Outsource to Experts—Expert Sourcing: How to Do Anything Without Knowing How
第三部分 付款：不要把夢想變成工作 - PART III Payments: Don’t Turn Dreams into Jobs
第七章 改變你的薪資方式，改變你的生活：如何創造經濟護城河 - Chapter 7 Change How You’re Paid, Change Your Life: How to Create an Economic Moat
第八章 珍惜你的時間，不要計時你的價值觀：如何在你想做什麼的時候做你想做的事 - Chapter 8 Value Your Time, Don’t Time Your Values: How to Do What You Want When You Want
第四部分 多產作品：超越目標、習慣與優勢，而非落後 - PART IV Prolifics: Get Beyond Goals, Habits, and Strengths, Not Behind
第九章 創造棱鏡般的生產力：如何選擇小投入，創造一系列重大成果 - Chapter 9 Create Prismic Productivity: How to Choose Small Inputs That Create an Array of Big Results
第十章 提出更好的問題，獲得更好的答案：如何快速升級你的想法 - Chapter 10 Ask Better Questions, Get Better Answers: How to Quickly Upgrade Your Thoughts
後記：要多產，而非完美 - Epilogue: Be Prolific, Not Perfect

chapters.title: 題詞 - Epigraph
chapters.excerpt: I arise in the morning torn between a desire to improve (or save) the world and a desire to enjoy (or savor) the world. This makes it hard to plan the day. 我早晨醒來時，內心掙扎於改善（或拯救）世界與享 受（或細細品味）世界的渴望之間。這讓規劃一天變 得困難。 —E. B. WHITE ——E. B. 懷特

chapters.title: 前言 時間 = 今天是我的一切 - Preface TIME = Today Is My Everything
chapters.excerpt: PREFACE 前言 TIME = Today Is My Everything TIME = 今天是我的一切 Gavin’s Law: Live to Start. Start to Live. 加文定律：從一開始就活著。開始活出去。 “BALLISTIC MISSILE THREAT TO HAWAII 「夏威夷遭受彈道飛彈威脅 There is a Ballistic missile 有彈道飛彈 THREAT INBOUND TO HAWAII. 威脅正向夏威夷逼近。 Seek Immediate shelter and wait for future information.” 立即尋求庇護，並等待未來消息。」 The text message appeared on my phone while standing in a friend’s living room thousands of miles away from my home and family in Hawaii. 這則簡訊出現在我手機上，當時我站在朋友的客廳裡，距離我在夏威 夷的家和家人千里之外。 I called my wife. 我打電話給我太太。 No answer. 沒有回應。 I called my three boys. 我打給我的三個兒子。 No answer. 沒有回應。 No answer. 沒有回應。 No answer. 沒有回應。 “THIS IS NOT A TEST. Reply with YES to confirm.” 「這不是考驗。請回覆「是」以確認。」 I called again, and my thirteen-year-old son, Cardon, answered and then stammered panicked, tearful good-byes, certain he would be dead in a matter of moments. 我又打了一次電話，我十三歲的兒子卡登接起，然後結結巴巴地說著 驚慌和淚眼的告別，確信自己很快就會死去。 “I love you so much, Dad.” 「我好愛你，爸爸。」 I sat down where I had been standing, staring off into space. 我坐回原地，目光茫然。 My teenage son had just said his good-byes. 我十幾歲的兒子剛剛道別。 I was powerless even to comfort them. Sorrow surged through my body, as this wasn’t the first time I had felt powerless. Like a wheel going ’round, my world was crashing down again. 我甚至無法安慰他們。悲傷湧上心頭，這不是我第一次感到無力。就 像輪子轉動一樣，我的世界又開始崩塌。 In brief flashes, my mind ricocheted from one tragedy to the next tragedy to the next tragedy to the next… a long list of experiences that had brought my family and me to this moment. 在短暫的閃回中，我

chapters.title: 導言：為什麼要反時間管理？ - Introduction Why Anti-Time Management?
chapters.excerpt: INTRODUCTION 介绍 Why Anti-Time Management? 為什麼是反時間管理？ Time management is a seductive promise. 時間管理是一個誘人的承諾。 It sounds like you’ll have more time if you apply the principles of time management. Yet… nothing could be further from the truth. 聽起來如果你運用時間管理的原則，會有更多時間。然而......事實遠 非如此。 Why Do You Have Less Time Under Time Management? 為什麼你在時間管理上的時間比較少？ The dictionary definition of the word management literally means “control.” Time management means “time control.” However, whose time is being controlled, by whom, and when is what matters to Time Tippers. Time management does not mean you have control over your time. Time management usually means your time is controlled by someone else. 字典中「 管理 」一詞的字面意思是「控制」。 時間管理就是「時間 控制」。然而，時間被誰、誰、何時掌控，才是時間倒數者最重要 的。時間管理並不代表你能掌控自己的時間。時間管理通常意味著你 的時間會被別人控制。 Time management means you don’t have control of your time. 時間管理意味著你無法掌控自己的時間。 Let me be clear. 讓我說清楚。 Time management has nothing to do with you controlling your own time. 時間管理跟你控制自己的時間無關。 Period. 就這樣。 Industry deliberately designed time management so a manager at work could control you and your time, what you do, where, and when. 業界刻意設計時間管理，讓工作經理能控制你和你的時間，決定你做 什麼、在哪裡、何時做。 Welcome to your run-of-the-mill job. 歡迎來到你那份普通的工作 。 In fact, your vacation time, the hours you work, your breaks, when you retire (or not), and where and when you do your work— even if it’s from home—are carefully calculated factors of time management (and often paired with a reward system of titles, roles, bonuses, employee of the

chapters.title: 第一章 讓最終因成為你的首因：如何選擇何時做什麼 - Chapter 1 Make Final Cause Your First Cause: How to Choose What to Do and When
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 1 第一章 Make Final Cause Your First Cause 把最終原因當作你的首要目標 How to Choose What to Do and When 如何選擇何時做什麼 It is vital that we are equipped with the humility to understand that changing the world and keeping innovation alive require that we change ourselves. 我們必須具備謙卑的心態，明白改變世界與保持創新 活力，必須改變自己。 —WHITNEY JOHNSON, bestselling author of Disrupt Yourself —— 惠特妮·約翰遜， 暢銷書《Disrupt Yourself》 作者 Sirah spent much of her youth on the streets, homeless, in gangs, addicted, and abused. “I grew up with a medicine man, and we would sacrifice an animal, do a prayer around it, and eat it for like a month. There were some good skills that I gained, but there were a lot of weird things going down, a lot of sexual abuse, a lot of neglect,” she told me. Growing up, she’d been kidnapped, raped, and left for dead more times than she’d like to remember. Sirah 童年大部分時間流浪街頭，無家可歸、加入幫派、成癮和遭受 虐待。「我從小有個巫醫，我們會祭祀動物，圍著牠祈禱，然後吃牠 一個月。我學到了一些不錯的技能，但也發生了很多奇怪的事情，很 多性虐待，很多被忽視，」她告訴我。成長過程中，她被綁架、強 暴、被遺棄的次數比她願意回想的還多。 Time and circumstance never were Sirah’s friends. 時間與環境從來不是西拉的朋友。 “I dropped out of the fourth grade,” she said. “I would have to go sell my Nintendo to get enough money for my dad to shoot heroin.… I was left alone for long periods of time.” Her dad died of an overdose when she was young. 「我四年級就輟學了，」她說。「我得去賣掉我的任天堂，才能賺夠 錢讓我爸打海洛因......我被獨自留下很長時間。」她爸爸在她年幼時 因藥物過量去世。 “I was just going through my report cards from school the other day,” she told me. As her elementary school teacher reported: 「我前幾天在翻學校的成績單，」她告訴我。正如她的

chapters.title: 第二章 為彈性而非平衡工作：如何掌控你的時間 - Chapter 2 Work for Flexibility, Not Balance: How to Own Your Time
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 2 第二章 Work for Flexibility, Not Balance 追求彈性，而非平衡 How to Own Your Time 如何擁有你的時間 Too often, we forget that our professional lives can, and should, be joyful. 我們常常忘記，我們的職業生涯本可以且應該充滿喜 悅。 —DORIE CLARK, bestselling author of Stand Out —— 多莉·克拉克 ，暢銷書《 脫穎而出 》作者 Doug started working for a very large US investment bank in New York City. He worked in highly quantitative groups but stood out for communication, problem-solving, and interpersonal skills. This led to quickly increasing both his income and his responsibilities. Ultimately, Doug got a large promotion to managing director and was asked to relocate from New York City to Paris with his family. 道格開始在紐約市一家大型美國投資銀行工作。他曾在高度量化的團 隊工作，但以溝通、解決問題和人際交往能力脫穎而出。這使他的收 入和責任迅速增加。最終，道格獲得重大晉升為董事總經理，並被邀 請與家人從紐約市搬到巴黎。 Doug said, “We jumped at the chance to live internationally and have our young kids learn a second language. But here’s the thing— promotions at investment banks are like pie-eating contests where the first-place prize is more pie. Not only do you have to love pie, but pie is the only thing you can love. While I had more responsibilities, it didn’t always come with more resources, and the hours and scope were unrelenting.” Doug 說：「我們立刻抓住機會，能夠在國際間生活，並讓我們的年 幼孩子學習第二語言。但重點是——投資銀行的升遷就像吃派比賽， 第一名的獎品是更多的派。你不僅要愛派，派是你唯一能愛的東西。 雖然我有更多責任，但資源並不總是多，工時和工作範圍也非常緊 湊。」 He continued, “There’s a morbid joke within the industry that the job is a golden cage—the pay, prestige, etc., look amazing to those on the outside, but for the people in the job

chapters.title: 第三章 建造城堡，然後建造護城河：如何釋放你的時間並保護它 - Chapter 3 Build the Castle, Then the Moat: How to Free Up Your Time and Then Protect It
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 3 第三章 Build the Castle, Then the Moat 先蓋城堡，再建護城河 How to Free Up Your Time and Then Protect It 如何釋放你的時間，然後保護它 Love and success, always in that order. It’s that simple AND that difficult. 愛與成功，總是依序排列。事情就是這麼簡單，也就 是這麼困難。 —MISTER ROGERS ——羅傑斯先生 Sam Jones started a custom clothing business in 2014 after reading The Power of Starting Something Stupid with the dream of having more freedom to travel and spend time with his family and friends. Without knowing it, the business started running him. His relationships and freedom took a backseat as he made himself busy doing things he hated so that “one day” he would have that freedom. Sam Jones 於 2014 年在閱讀 《The Power of Starting Something Stupid》後，開始了一家訂製服裝事業，夢想能有更多自由旅行，並 與家人朋友共度時光。不知不覺中，公司開始操控他。他的人際關係 和自由都退居次要，他讓自己忙著做那些他討厭的事，只為了「有一 天」能擁有那份自由。 He took steps in the right direction when he started, but when it came down to it, he felt like he had built the wrong type of business because he had no time. He found an opportunity to sell the business and start from scratch again. 他剛開始時朝著正確的方向邁進，但說到底，他覺得自己建立錯了事 業類型，因為他沒有時間。他找到機會出售公司，重新開始。 Freedom was the goal, not the business. 自由才是目標，不是生意。 After selling the business, he reached out to me, and I pointed out that he was too busy building a moat before building the castle. He’d changed the nature of his work but not the order of his priorities. 賣掉生意後，他聯絡我，我指出他忙著建造護城河，還沒蓋城堡。他 改變了工作性質，但優先順序並未改變。 Sam said: 山姆說： I thought the store was my dream. I quickly learned that I was building barriers t

chapters.title: 第四章 堆疊你的專案——專案堆疊：如何讓時間為你工作 - Chapter 4 Stack Your Projects—Project Stacking: How to Make Time Work for You
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 4 第四章 Stack Your Projects—Project Stacking 堆疊你的專案——專案堆疊 How to Make Time Work for You 如何讓時間為你而設 Consider thyself to be dead, and to have completed thy life up to the present time; and live according to nature the remainder which is allowed thee. 認為你已死，並完成了你至今的生命;並依照自然而 活，剩下的部分是被允許的。 —MARCUS AURELIUS, Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher —— 馬庫斯·奧勒留， 羅馬皇帝及斯多葛哲學家 As an immigrant to America, Ben Willson came with the dream of freedom. He became an entrepreneur, and as each business grew, so did his responsibilities—working eighteen-hour days. 作為移民，班·威爾森懷抱自由的夢想來到美國。他成為了一名企業 家，隨著每一家企業成長，他的責任也隨之增加——每天工作十八小 時。 He said, “At first it didn’t feel like work, but very quickly that faded. With more clients came more problems, and I became overwhelmed. I slid down the slide of depression into the darkest moments of my life. I no longer wanted to be in business. I wanted someone to fire me from the job I had built for myself. I let the business slowly burn away.” 他說：「一開始感覺不像是在工作，但很快就消失了。客戶越多，問 題越多，我感到不堪負荷。我滑落了憂鬱的滑梯，進入人生最黑暗的 時刻。我不再想做生意了。我希望有人能把我從自己建立的工作中解 雇。我讓生意慢慢燒掉了。」 Ben learned about the joy of Time Tipping and broke the patterns he had developed early in his life. Ben said, “I wanted to be happy. I wanted to wake up feeling like I was helping others in their businesses and be a champion for entrepreneurship. I learned how to rethink how I spend my time building a business. I learned how to spot what would move the needle and how to avoid time- sucking activities. As my mindset shif

chapters.title: 第五章 同步你的工作——工作同步：如何將工作與生活同步 - Chapter 5 Sync Your Work—Work Syncing: How to Sync Your Work with Your Life
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 5 第五章 Sync Your Work—Work Syncing 同步你的工作——工作同步 How to Sync Your Work with Your Life 如何將你的工作與生活同步 You have to look at a total life, not just your professional life, your personal life. It’s a total life and all of the pieces have to snap to grid. 你必須看整體人生，不只是你的職業生活，還有個人 生活。這是一條完整的生命，所有碎片都必須吸附到 格子上。 —AICHA EVANS, CEO of Zoox and head of Amazon Automation ——AICHA EVANS，Zoox 執行長兼亞馬遜自動化主 管 Their two-seater prop plane ran out of gas in a remote area over northern Arizona. The sun had set, and the light grew dimmer by the minute. 他們的雙座螺旋槳飛機在亞利桑那州北部偏遠地區耗盡燃油。太陽已 經下山，光線一分一秒地暗淡下來。 It was the 1940s, and my grandma and grandpa piloted a joyride. They loved flying together as a young couple. Only, this flight had a problem. The gas leaked, and they had nowhere to land. 那是1940年代，我奶奶和爺爺駕駛著一場飆車。他們年輕時熱愛一起 飛行。只是，這班航班出了問題。瓦斯洩漏，他們無處降落。 It was too dark to land safely—even if they could find a field between the pines. 天色太暗，無法安全降落——即使他們能找到松樹間的田野。 They noticed a one-off restaurant in the distance. 他們注意到遠處有一家獨一無二的餐廳。 They had an idea. 他們有個主意。 What if they radioed and asked the customers to line their cars along the tiny dirt road with their headlights on so they could land? And that’s precisely what happened. 如果他們用無線電請顧客把車排在狹窄的土路上，開著頭燈降落呢？ 而這正是發生的事。 I think it’s safe to say that I wouldn’t be here had they not been so fortunate and resourceful. 我想可以肯定地說，如果不是他們這麼幸運且足智多謀，我不會在這 裡。 Synchronization Requires Resourcefulness 同步需要資源運用 Sometimes we forget how resourceful we can be until there is an eme

chapters.title: 第六章 外包給專家——專家採購：如何在不懂的情況下做任何事 - Chapter 6 Outsource to Experts—Expert Sourcing: How to Do Anything Without Knowing How
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 6 第六章 Outsource to Experts—Expert Sourcing 外包給專家——專家採購 How to Do Anything Without Knowing How 怎麼做任何事卻不會做 If you know who you are, you’ll see where you need to get better and where you might even need to hire someone else who is better than you. 如果你知道自己是誰，你就會知道哪些地方需要改 進，甚至可能需要聘請比你更優秀的人。 —ALISA COHN, executive coach and author of From Start-Up to Grown-Up ——ALISA COHN， 執行教練及《 從創業到成熟》 一書作者 There are ships buried under San Francisco. 舊金山地下埋藏著船隻。 Thousands of subway riders unsuspectingly travel through a three-masted ship named the Rome near Market Street every day. “The Rome had arrived in San Francisco’s bustling Gold Rush port in early 1850 with a full load of eager ‘49ers’ headed to find gold in the hills, as well as a cargo hold laden with bottles of ale and salt pork.” The gold rush brought droves of people from all corners of the world to California, becoming the largest mass migration in US history at the time, and brought a ton of ships. 每天，數千名地鐵乘客毫無戒心地乘坐一艘名為羅馬號的三桅船，停 靠在市場街附近。「 羅馬號於 1850 年初抵達舊金山熱鬧的淘金熱港 口，滿載熱情的『49 人』，前往山區尋找黃金，還有一個裝滿啤酒 瓶和鹽豬肉的貨艙。」淘金熱吸引了來自世界各地的大批人潮湧入加 州，成為當時美國史上最大規模的大規模移民潮，並帶來了大量船 隻。 San Francisco Bay quickly became known as a “forest of masts.” Ship captains and crew were searching for gold. The goods the captains brought on the ships became increasingly difficult to get from the deep water to the shore, so politicians decided to bring the shoreline closer to the ships by selling water lots if the purchasers would fill them with land. 舊金山灣迅速被稱為「桅杆森林」

chapters.title: 第七章 改變你的薪資方式，改變你的生活：如何創造經濟護城河 - Chapter 7 Change How You’re Paid, Change Your Life: How to Create an Economic Moat
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 7 第七章 Change How You’re Paid, Change Your Life 改變你的薪資方式，改變你的人生 How to Create an Economic Moat 如何打造經濟護城河 The art of getting rich consists not in industry, much less in saving, but in a better order, in timeliness, in being at the right spot. 致富的藝術不在於勤奮，更別說儲蓄，而是在於更好 的秩序、及時的把握，以及身處正確的位置。 —RALPH WALDO EMERSON —— 拉爾夫·瓦爾多·愛默生 Laura Wieck says she was “a burnt-out massage therapist with fertility issues.” Laura Wieck 說她是「一位有生育問題的精疲力竭按摩治療師」。 She was trying to figure out a way to not trade time for dollars. She says, “The best massage therapists are often the ones who spend extra time with their clients (often for free), provide amazing tools and resources for clients, and have a natural intuitive ability to help people ‘connect the dots’ between what is going on in their clients’ bodies with what is going on in their lives.” She wanted to help massage therapists have ease and freedom in their businesses, and “that could never be found in the ‘time for money’ structure.” 她試著想辦法不用時間換錢。她說：「最優秀的按摩師往往是那些願 意花更多時間陪伴客戶（通常是免費的）、提供優秀工具和資源，並 且擁有天生直覺能力，幫助人們『連結』客戶身體狀況與生活狀況的 人。」她希望幫助按摩治療師在事業上擁有輕鬆與自由，而「這種自 由在『有錢的時間』結構中永遠找不到。」 She asked herself if she could teach massage therapists how to incorporate coaching into their practices in what she calls the BodyMind Method©. Would they have a framework to work less, earn more, and help their clients get even better results? 她問自己是否能教導按摩治療師如何將教練融入他們的實踐，並稱之 為「身心法」（Body-Mind Method©）。他們是否有一套框架，能減 少工作時間、提高收入，並幫助客戶獲得更好的成果？ Since Laura started the BodyMi

chapters.title: 第八章 珍惜你的時間，不要計時你的價值觀：如何在你想做什麼的時候做你想做的事 - Chapter 8 Value Your Time, Don’t Time Your Values: How to Do What You Want When You Want
chapters.excerpt: CHAPTER 8 第八章 Value Your Time, Don’t Time Your Values 珍惜你的時間，不要計時你的價值 How to Do What You Want When You Want 如何隨時隨地做你想做的事 I’ve become convinced that time management is not a solution—it’s actually part of the problem. 我已經深信，時間管理不是解決方法——它其實是問 題的一部分。 —ADAM GRANT, organizational psychologist ——亞當·格蘭特， 組織心理學家 When I was sixteen, I really wanted to start making my own money. I thought the best way to do that in my small town was to get a job at the grocery store or gas station or pick up trash at the county fair. I told my dad I wanted a job, and to my surprise he said, “You don’t want a job.” I asked why not, thinking that getting a job was a super-responsible thing to do. He told me that I’d be working my whole life and right now I should focus on school and having fun. But I persisted and explained I wanted to be able to make and spend my own money for more freedom. 我十六歲時，真的很想開始自己賺錢。我覺得在我這個小鎮上，最好 的方式就是去超市、加油站打工，或是在縣市集撿垃圾。我告訴我爸 我想找工作，令我驚訝的是，他說：「你不想要工作。」我問為什麼 不，覺得找工作是非常負責任的事。他說我一輩子都要工作，現在應 該專心念書和享受生活。但我堅持說我想自己賺錢並花錢，爭取更多 自由。 He then came up with the most random plan. 接著他想出了一個非常隨機的計畫。 He told me to go to the watermelon farms and ask if I could buy all of the irregular-size and -shaped watermelons. He said that the farms couldn’t sell them to the grocery stores. They’d go rotten and be thrown away. 他叫我去西瓜農場，問問能不能買下所有大小不規則、形狀不規則的 西瓜。他說農場不能賣給雜貨店。它們會腐爛然後被丟棄。 My dad gave me “seed money” to go and negotiate for the watermelons. My younger brother, Erik, and I drove to the farms in El Cent
